commands.review_summary:
The PR pins CodexBar status-item menus and descriptor submenus to `NSApp.effectiveAppearance`, adds a focused status-menu appearance test, and updates the unreleased changelog.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source inspection supports the reported path because current main creates status-item menus and descriptor submenus without assigning `NSMenu.appearance`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should wait for contributor-supplied real UI proof; automation cannot prove the contributor's macOS appearance setup for them.

target.title: Pin status-bar menus to the system appearance (fix dark menu in Light mode)
target.body:
## Problem

The menu-bar dropdown renders dark even when macOS is set to **Light** mode. `NSStatusItem` menus don't follow the system Light/Dark setting — they inherit the *menu bar's* vibrant appearance, which macOS turns dark whenever a dark or strongly-colored window/wallpaper sits behind the menu bar. The whole overview popover (Überblick / Codex / Claude) then shows up dark against an otherwise light system.

(Apps that present an `NSPopover` panel instead of an `NSMenu` don't hit this, which is why other menu-bar apps stay light next to CodexBar.)

## Fix

Pin each status-bar `NSMenu.appearance` to `NSApp.effectiveAppearance` so the dropdown follows the user's actual Light/Dark setting:

- new helper `pinMenuToSystemAppearance(_:)` (compares by appearance `name`, only reassigns on change);
- applied in `makeBaseMenu()` (first paint of root/provider menus);
- applied at the top of `menuWillOpen(_:)` before any early return, so every menu + delegate submenu is covered and it re-syncs if the user switches appearance while the app keeps a menu alive;
- applied to the one provider-switch submenu created without a delegate.

This also makes the existing light-mode handling in the provider switcher (which already keys off `effectiveAppearance`) fire correctly in this scenario.

## Testing

`swift build` compiles the changed target cleanly (every CodexBar source, including the edited file, builds with zero errors). I could not complete a full app/bundle build locally because the `@Entry` / `#Preview` macros need Xcode's macro plugins, which aren't present in a Command-Line-Tools-only toolchain — those failures are confined to `MenuHighlightStyle.swift` and the `KeyboardShortcuts` dependency and are unrelated to this change.

Added a CHANGELOG entry under `0.34.1 — Unreleased`.
target.author: npapridonu
